title: Weekly Surge In Google Trends For Last Minute Flight Deals Signals Travel Demand Rebound
thesis: A spike in last minute flight deal searches indicates spontaneous travel intentions increasing soon. Rising last-minute travel demand benefits discretionary and airline-related stocks.
signal_logic: If weekly Google Trends for 'last minute flight deals' rises 30% WoW
entry_exit: Entry: If weekly Google Trends for 'last minute flight deals' rises 30% WoW Exit: After 4 weeks or if trend declines 15% WoW
position_sizing: Allocate 5% per signal, max 20% total exposure.
risks: Data source instability, false positives, and regime shifts.
implementation_notes: Use Google Trends weekly search interest via api (weekly).
reason_it_will_fire_in_30_days: Travel search interest often fluctuates seasonally and with promotions causing frequent spikes.

schema.idea: Weekly surge in Google Trends for 'last minute flight deals' signals travel demand rebound
schema.behavior: A spike in last minute flight deal searches indicates spontaneous travel intentions increasing soon.

market_impact.rationale: Rising last-minute travel demand benefits discretionary and airline-related stocks.

trade_logic.entry: If weekly Google Trends for 'last minute flight deals' rises 30% WoW
trade_logic.exit: After 4 weeks or if trend declines 15% WoW

schema.reason_it_will_fire_in_30_days: Travel search interest often fluctuates seasonally and with promotions causing frequent spikes.
